title: GitHub Auth, Resume & Tags Table Accessibility
description: Fix high-priority issues: add accessible loading announcement to GithubCallback spinner, convert SettingsResumeSection div role='button' drop zone to native button element, add semantic caption to Tags table, improve SharePipeline loading spinner label specificity, remove redundant aria-labels from OfferComparison buttons, and verify KanbanBoard drag-and-drop has screen reader announcements.

userStories.title: Add role='status' aria-live to GithubCallback loading spinner
userStories.description: GithubCallback.jsx renders a loading spinner (~lines 37-39) while authenticating with GitHub, but the spinner has no accessible announcement. Screen reader users land on this page with no context about what is happening. Add role='status' aria-live='polite' and aria-label='Authenticating with GitHub' to the spinner container div so screen readers announce the loading state.
acceptanceCriteria: GithubCallback.jsx spinner container has role='status' and aria-live='polite'
The container has aria-label='Authenticating with GitHub' or equivalent text
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Convert SettingsResumeSection ResumeDropZone div to native button
userStories.description: SettingsResumeSection.jsx ResumeDropZone (~lines 89-100) uses <div role='button' tabIndex={0}> instead of a native <button> element. Using a div requires manually implementing all keyboard behavior that buttons provide natively. Replace the div root with a <button type='button'> element, remove role='button' and tabIndex={0}' (redundant on button), and preserve all existing onClick, onKeyDown, className, and other attributes.
acceptanceCriteria: SettingsResumeSection.jsx ResumeDropZone root element is a <button type='button'>
role='button' and tabIndex={0} are removed
All existing event handlers and className are preserved
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add semantic caption to Tags TagsTable instead of aria-label
userStories.description: Tags.jsx TagsTable component uses aria-label='Tags' on the <table> element (~line 128). The preferred semantic approach for table titles is a <caption> element inside the table, which is more broadly supported by assistive technologies. Replace the aria-label with a <caption className='sr-only'>Tags management</caption> as the first child of the table.
acceptanceCriteria: Tags.jsx table has a <caption className='sr-only'> element instead of aria-label
The aria-label attribute is removed from the table
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Improve SharePipeline Loader2 aria-label specificity
userStories.description: SharePipeline.jsx renders a Loader2 spinning icon (~line 119) with a generic aria-label='Loading'. This doesn't communicate what is loading. Update the aria-label to 'Loading share settings' or wrap the spinner in an aria-live='polite' region that announces context-specific loading status.
acceptanceCriteria: SharePipeline.jsx Loader2 icon has a specific aria-label (e.g., 'Loading share settings') instead of generic 'Loading'
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Remove redundant aria-labels from OfferComparison buttons with visible text
userStories.description: OfferComparison.jsx has two buttons with redundant aria-labels that differ from their visible text: the ErrorState retry button (~line 55) has aria-label='Retry loading offers' while displaying 'Try again', and the export button (~line 236) has aria-label='Export as PDF' while displaying 'Export PDF'. When aria-label and button text differ, screen readers announce the aria-label but sighted users see different text. Remove the aria-label from both buttons and let their visible text serve as the accessible name.
acceptanceCriteria: OfferComparison.jsx ErrorState retry button does not have a redundant aria-label
OfferComparison.jsx export button does not have a redundant aria-label
Both buttons retain their visible text which serves as the accessible name
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add drag-and-drop accessibility announcements to KanbanBoard
userStories.description: KanbanBoard.jsx implements drag-and-drop for application cards using dnd-kit. Screen reader users cannot perceive visual drag operations. Read KanbanBoard.jsx and add an aria-live='polite' region or use DndContext's accessibility announcements (via getMovementAnnouncement or screenReaderInstructions) to announce when a card is picked up, moved, and dropped, providing context like 'Application moved to Interview stage'.
acceptanceCriteria: KanbanBoard.jsx provides screen reader announcements for drag-and-drop operations
Either uses DndContext accessibility props or an aria-live='assertive' region for drag state changes
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-hidden to ApiErrorMessage decorative icon
userStories.description: ApiErrorMessage.jsx renders an error state component that likely includes a decorative icon (such as an alert triangle or X circle) alongside error text. Read ApiErrorMessage.jsx and add aria-hidden='true' to any decorative icon elements. Also verify the component has role='alert' or aria-live='polite' for dynamic error announcements.
acceptanceCriteria: ApiErrorMessage.jsx decorative icons have aria-hidden='true'
The error container has role='alert' or aria-live='polite' if not already present
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add accessible name to ErrorBoundary fallback UI
userStories.description: ErrorBoundary.jsx renders a fallback UI when an uncaught error occurs. The fallback likely includes an error message, and possibly a reload or retry button. Read ErrorBoundary.jsx and ensure: (1) the fallback has a <h1> or <h2> heading announcing the error, (2) any icon-only retry button has an aria-label, (3) the error container has role='alert' to announce the error to screen readers automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: ErrorBoundary.jsx fallback UI has a semantic heading element
ErrorBoundary.jsx fallback container has role='alert'
Any icon-only buttons have aria-label attributes
Build passes with no errors
